Details:
Data are suggestive that ultraviolet (UV) irradiation is effective for the removal of acetochlor. Based on data from one study, a range of 77 to 97 percent photodegradation was observed with the addition of 1 to 10 mg/L of humic acid. The control scenario, with no humic acid added, resulted in 71 percent removal. The author suggested that humic acids (as well as fulvic acids and some inorganic ions) could sensitize photodegradation up to a certain concentration before having a counteractive effect by absorbing/blocking the light energy. However, another study found that the most effective scenario was the control scenario, removing 62 percent of the acetochlor present, with increasing amounts of humic acids reducing the effects of UV. 20 mg/L of humic acid resulted in 44 percent removal.
April 2009
